content.cn_text: 第四部分。
content.en_text: Section 4.

content.cn_text: 你将听到关于催产素和信任的讲座片段。
content.en_text: You will hear parts of a lecture about oxytocin and trust.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间来查看问题31到40。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 你有一些时间来查看关于催产素和信任的讲座。
content.en_text: You have some time to look at a lecture about oxytocin and trust.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题31至40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 信任的发展是一种重要的社会工具，它使人们能够在专业和个人层面建立富有成效的
content.en_text: The development of trust is an essential social tool, allowing people to form productive

content.cn_text: 和有意义的关系。
content.en_text: and meaningful relationships both at a professional and personal level.

content.cn_text: 信任在友谊、爱情、家庭和组织中不可或缺，并发挥着关键作用，
content.en_text: Trust is indispensable in friendship, love, families and organisations and plays a key

content.cn_text: 在经济交流和政治中。
content.en_text: role in economic exchange and politics.

content.cn_text: 许多最新证据表明，信任有助于经济、政治和社会成功。
content.en_text: Much recent evidence indicates that trust contributes to economic, political and social success.

content.cn_text: 当我们与陌生人接触时，例如与我们第一次见面的客户做生意时，
content.en_text: When we make contacts with strangers such as doing business with the clients who we meet

content.cn_text: 我们对他们的信任只能依赖于他们的善意。
content.en_text: for the first time, our trust in them can only rely on their kindness.

content.cn_text: 因此，从我们自己的判断，以及来自他人的所有评论中，我们可以对
content.en_text: So from our own judgement, all the comments from other people, we can have a rough idea

content.cn_text: 陌生人是否值得信任有一个大致的了解。
content.en_text: if the stranger is trustworthy.

content.cn_text: 信任很重要，因为它是所有人际关系发展的基础。
content.en_text: Trust is important because it is the basis around which all human relationships evolve.

content.cn_text: 没有信任，就不可能存在关系。
content.en_text: Without trust, there can be no relationship.

content.cn_text: 信任可以自然产生，也可以被显现出来。
content.en_text: Trust can come naturally or it can be manifested.

content.cn_text: 如果你在生活中找到如此程度的信任，那么你是幸运的。
content.en_text: If you find trust of this magnitude in life, then you are lucky.

content.cn_text: 此外，你总能指望他们保护你度过生命中的所有（如果不是大部分）危险。
content.en_text: Also, you can always count on them to protect through all, if not most, of life's perils.

content.cn_text: 信任很重要，因为如果你不信任某人，那么他们就不可用。
content.en_text: Trust is important because if you don't trust someone, then they are not available.

content.cn_text: 信不信由你，减少遭受贫困的机会在于理解信任，
content.en_text: Believe it or not, reducing the chance of suffering from poverty is to understand trust

content.cn_text: 因为当你信任他人时，你可以获得更多的支持和资源。
content.en_text: because when you trust other people, you can have more support and resources.

content.cn_text: 然而，我们信任他人的能力因人而异，就像不同
content.en_text: However, our ability to trust others varies from individual to individual, just like different

content.cn_text: 人识别他人面孔的能力不同。
content.en_text: people's ability of recognising a person's face is not the same.

content.cn_text: 信任的纽带也极其脆弱。
content.en_text: Bonds of trust are also extremely fragile.

content.cn_text: 一次背叛行为，例如婚外情，可以瞬间抹去多年值得信任的
content.en_text: A single act of betrayal, such as a marital affair, can instantly erase years of trustworthy

content.cn_text: 行为。
content.en_text: behaviour.

content.cn_text: 这种信任破裂的后果可能是灾难性的，而且不仅仅是对一段关系。
content.en_text: The consequences of such breaches in confidence can be disastrous and not only for a relationship.

content.cn_text: 这项研究表明，催产素如何在信任被破坏后促进社会互动，
content.en_text: The study demonstrates how oxytocin can facilitate social interactions after trust has been violated

content.cn_text: 通过潜在地降低与社会风险相关的防御机制，并通过克服
content.en_text: by potentially lowering defence mechanisms associated with social risks and by overcoming

content.cn_text: 对未来行为适应至关重要的负面反馈。
content.en_text: negative feedback that is important for adapting behaviour in the future.

content.cn_text: 幸运的是，我们体内的催产素具有激发人们之间信任的能力。
content.en_text: Luckily, oxytocin in our body has the ability to inspire trust in and among people.

content.cn_text: 每当人与人之间发生互动时，这种化学物质就会被释放。
content.en_text: This chemical is released whenever interaction happens between people.

content.cn_text: 为了研究社会互动，经济学家和最近的神经科学家利用了
content.en_text: To study social interactions, economists and more recently neuroscientists take advantage

content.cn_text: 一个简单的实验。
content.en_text: of a simple experiment.

content.cn_text: 两名参与者玩所谓的信任游戏。
content.en_text: Two participants play the so-called Trust Game.

content.cn_text: 第一位参与者面临一个决定：是保留一笔钱，还是与
content.en_text: The first participant is faced with a decision to keep a sum of money or share it with the

content.cn_text: 第二位参与者分享，而第二位参与者没有义务归还这笔钱。
content.en_text: second participant who has no obligation to give the money back.

content.cn_text: 如果第二个人归还，他会得到更多的钱。
content.en_text: He would get more money if the second person returns it.

content.cn_text: 在这个游戏中，第一个人面临着一个重要的社会困境：信任还是不信任。
content.en_text: In this game, the first person is left with an important social dilemma to trust or not

content.cn_text: 信任。
content.en_text: to trust.

content.cn_text: 尽管信任更有利可图，但这样做会使投资者面临被背叛的风险。
content.en_text: Although it is more profitable to trust, doing so leaves the investor at risk of betrayal.

content.cn_text: 信任并非两人初次见面时自然产生的东西。
content.en_text: Trust is not something that naturally occurs when two people meet for the first time.

content.cn_text: 它涉及一个过程。
content.en_text: It involves a process.

content.cn_text: 一个人必须向另一个人展示值得信任的品质。
content.en_text: One person has to show the quality of being trustworthy to the other person.

content.cn_text: 这可以通过某些行为和沟通来实现。
content.en_text: This can be achieved by certain behaviours and communication.

content.cn_text: 因此，信任是有条件的。
content.en_text: Therefore, trust is conditional.

content.cn_text: 人们不应将其视为理所当然。
content.en_text: People should not take it for granted.

content.cn_text: 催产素不仅存在于人类。
content.en_text: Oxytocin does not only exist in human beings.

content.cn_text: 动物也有这种天赋，当它们彼此之间有合作时就能看到。
content.en_text: Animals also have this gift and it can be seen when they have cooperation with each other.

content.cn_text: 例如，猴子体内的催产素使它们能够共同寻找食物并对抗
content.en_text: Oxytocin in monkeys, for example, allow them to work together to find food and to fight

content.cn_text: 敌人。
content.en_text: against enemies.

content.cn_text: 根据纳什均衡博弈论，第一个参与者会做出最佳决定，
content.en_text: The Nash Equilibrium's Game Theory, the first participant will make the best decision

content.cn_text: 他能做到的，并且不期望第二个参与者会改变他的决定。
content.en_text: he can, with no expectations that the second participant would change his decision.

content.cn_text: 第二个参与者也会这样做。
content.en_text: The second participant will do the same.

content.cn_text: 因此，结果取决于对方的决定。
content.en_text: So the outcome depends on the decision of the other person.

content.cn_text: 这个游戏与我们刚刚讨论的类似。
content.en_text: This game is similar to the one we just talked about.

content.cn_text: 一个人体内的催产素水平不会保持不变。
content.en_text: The Oxytocin level doesn't stay the same in a person's body.

content.cn_text: 研究发现，承受巨大压力的人体内催产素水平较高。
content.en_text: Studies have found that people who are under a lot of stress would have high levels of

content.cn_text: 催产素。
content.en_text: oxytocin.

content.cn_text: 催产素导致人与人之间信任的大幅增加，从而大大增加了
content.en_text: Oxytocin causes a substantial increase in trust among humans, thereby greatly increasing

content.cn_text: 社会互动带来的益处。
content.en_text: the benefits from social interactions.

content.cn_text: 还值得注意的是，催产素对信任的影响并非由于普遍增加了
content.en_text: It's also noted that the effect of oxytocin on trust is not due to a general increase

content.cn_text: 承担风险的意愿。
content.en_text: in the readiness to bear risks.

content.cn_text: 相反，催产素专门影响个人接受因人际互动而产生的社会
content.en_text: On the contrary, oxytocin specifically affects an individual's willingness to accept social

content.cn_text: 风险的意愿。
content.en_text: risks arising through interpersonal interactions.

content.cn_text: 总而言之，催产素就像一种有助于社会联系的社会“胶水”。
content.en_text: After some up, oxytocin is like a kind of social glue that is helpful in social bonding.

content.cn_text: 需要进一步研究催产素和信任，以及这种激素如何用于
content.en_text: Further research is needed to explore oxytocin and trust, and how this hormone can be used

content.cn_text: 加强我们对其他人的信任，甚至治疗与社会行为缺陷特别相关的
content.en_text: to strengthen our trust in others and even treat mental disorders that specifically

content.cn_text: 精神障碍。
content.en_text: relate to deficiencies in social behaviour.

content.cn_text: 接下来我们将讨论...
content.en_text: Next we're going to talk about...

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4.

content.cn_text: 现在你有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
